title: Fix 2 failing beamr-dependent tests in the aion crate
task: Two tests in crates/aion/ fail because they depend on beamr runtime fixtures that aren't set up correctly. Fix them so cargo test -p aion passes 168/168. The tests are: runtime::handle::tests::registers_spawns_and_shuts_down (Os error 2: No such file or directory) and activity::dispatch::tests::failing_activity_surfaces_typed_error_with_trapped_exit (assertion on trapped exit message). These tests need the beamr VM to find BEAM bytecode fixtures. The test fixture crates/aion/tests/fixtures/aion_fixture_workflow.beam exists and other tests in the crate use it successfully — so the issue is likely a path or setup problem specific to these two tests.

requirements.description: Fix runtime::handle::tests::registers_spawns_and_shuts_down — the test gets Os error 2 (No such file or directory). Investigate what file path it's looking for and either fix the path or add the missing fixture.

Fix activity::dispatch::tests::failing_activity_surfaces_typed_error_with_trapped_exit — the assertion runtime.has_trapped_exit_message(workflow, activity) fails. This likely depends on R1 (the runtime handle needs to start correctly first).
Verify the full crate test suite passes after the fixes.
requirements.acceptance: cargo test -p aion passes with 0 failures

context: The aion crate at crates/aion/ embeds the beamr VM. Tests use RuntimeHandle::new() to start an embedded BEAM scheduler. The test fixture at crates/aion/tests/fixtures/aion_fixture_workflow.beam is pre-compiled Erlang bytecode. Other tests in the crate (e.g., engine::builder tests) pass, so the beamr integration works in general. The failing tests are in crates/aion/src/runtime/handle.rs and crates/aion/src/activity/dispatch.rs. The CLAUDE.md says beamr is a crates.io dependency — no separate binary needed.
